calendar.matters:
PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jul-14-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Theodore Hellman appeared by telephone for Executor.

The Court stated that it reviewed all of the papers and noted that the Probate Examiner raised some issues relating to the creditor's claims as well as some other issues. Mr. Hellman stated that there is one exhibit for each of the creditor's claims attached to the declaration supplementing the report, that the Decedent had a condition and was unable to care for himself, that the Decedent's only relatives are two Nephews, and that Andrew is a Nephew and a Claimant, and Susi the other Claimant is Andrew's Wife. Mr. Hellman also stated that the Decedent had caregivers, that Susi while assisting with gathering information for the Decedent's taxes found that the caregiver was double dipping, and that the Decedent then asked Susi to help him with care needs and bookkeeping. In addition, Mr. Hellman stated that the arrangement was to pay Susi at a rate of $35.00 an hour, that it came out to $2,000.00 to $2,500.00 per month and that Susi never kept track of her time. Furthermore, Mr. Hellman stated that Susi attended to the Decedent's hygiene, that she did the shopping and cooking, that the Decedent was moved into a facility in 2015, and that she then took on the responsibility of leasing the house and property management. Mr. Hellman also stated that the $400.00 per month amount was made residuary, that the Beneficiaries were aware of it and that the Beneficiaries have consented to the payment of Susi's claim. In addition, Mr. Hellman stated that the other claim is for the company/family business, that the family business was started by the Decedent's Father, that the Decedent ran of funds to provide for his care, that the company advanced funds for the Decedent's care and expenses, that it was intended that the company would get repaid and raised the issue of the payments being labeled as "Director's Fees" and perhaps being mis-classified. The Court finds that everything is in order. The Court granted the Amended Petition to Approve Status of Administration and Petition for Final Distribution, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
